highlights.title: Unified alert system with customizable glucose alarms
changes: New customizable glucose alarms include urgent‑low, low, high, forecasted low, and Carbs Required.
Each alarm can use its own threshold, schedule, sound, and snooze settings.
A shared Day/Night window controls when alarms are active and the Add‑Alarm picker prevents overlaps.
Trio shows an in‑app banner when foregrounded and sends iOS notifications when backgrounded, with unified snooze.
Alerts are organized by severity and Trio hides duplicates when a CGM app already provides the same alert.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1203: Refactor Trio Alert/Notification Handling – Prepare O5 and Libre 3/3+ Integration [1/3]

Introduce new alert system to Trio * Route LoopKit alerts through TrioAlertManager * BaseDeviceDataManager.issueAlert/retractAlert forward to new TrioAlertManager * Drop the AlertObserver auto-ack and pumpNotification broadcast * TrioAlertCategory enum + classifier covers Omni, Dana, Minimed     alert identifiers; .commsTransient and .algorithmError dwell-     suppressed (count=2 or 60s), everything else surfaces * APSManager.processError synthesises a LoopKit alert via the     classifier; manual bolus + cancelBolus catches emit explicit     .bolusFailed alerts so they always fire * SwiftUI in-app banner (iOS-push styling, SF Symbol on tinted     square, live timestamp, tap/swipe-to-ack, stacked deck with     critical-on-top, tap top to expand) * Drop notificationsPump/Cgm/Carb/Algorithm settings * Test alert buttons in PumpConfig (need to be removed later)

highlights.title: Move Snooze All to Settings and unify alerts
changes: Snooze All is now a button in Settings → Notifications instead of a hidden long-press on the glucose bobble.
Tapping Snooze All opens a sheet offering 15 min, 1 hr, 3 hrs, and 6 hrs snooze durations.
All in-app alerts now route through a single TrioAlertManager so alerts use a unified presentation.
Specific warnings (Garmin Connect missing, CGM open failures, stale calibration) now appear via the unified alert UI.
Info-level logger messages no longer surface as in-app banners and are log-only.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1243: Move "Snooze All" to Settings and Clean up Legacy Alerting; Follow-Up to #1203 – Prepare O5 and Libre 3/3+ Integration [3/3]

highlights.title: Fix snooze long-press behavior
changes: Long-pressing the CGM area now reliably opens the Snooze sheet.
Tapping the CGM area still opens CGM selection or the setup sheet as before.

highlights.title: Quick‑Pick Boluses via long‑press +
changes: Long‑press the + button on the home screen to open a Quick‑Pick Boluses sheet.
The app suggests up to five usual bolus amounts learned from your past 90 days and time‑of‑day patterns.
Tap a suggestion, then confirm and authorize (Face/Touch ID) to enact the bolus.
A new Treatments setting lets you enable or disable the Quick‑Pick Boluses feature.
If no suitable bolus history exists, the app shows a clear message explaining why no suggestions are available.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1248: Add a Quick Bolus feature
Add history-based quick bolus via long-press on + button

highlights.title: Default to Swift Oref; add 'Use JavaScript Oref' toggle
changes: Trio now uses the Swift Oref algorithm by default.
The advanced setting was renamed to "Use JavaScript Oref" and is off by default.
Turning on that setting switches Trio back to the legacy JavaScript algorithm.
Settings text and loop-status labels were updated to reflect Swift as the default.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1273: Use Swift Oref by default

items.title: Updated translations from Crowdin
changes: Updated translations throughout the app from Crowdin.
Norwegian (nb-NO) wording changed in many places from "blodsukker" to "glukose".
Added or updated localized UI labels such as the Device Alerts help label and some punctuation symbols.
Added localized relative time strings for hours, minutes, and seconds (e.g. "X hours ago").

items.title: Simpler markers for glucose points
changes: Glucose readings now use the chart's built-in circle symbol.
Manual glucose readings still display as a red drop marker.
Colored glucose points still follow the app's dynamic color scheme.
Users will not see any visual changes to the chart.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1274: Replace Image in GlucoseChartView with symbol

items.title: Add Time-Sensitive Notifications capability
changes: The app build configuration now includes the Time-Sensitive Notifications capability.
Provisioning/bundle ID setup will include permission for time-sensitive user notifications.

sources.title: feat(fastlane): Add UserNotificationsTimeSensitive capability to bundle ID

items.title: Build updates app capabilities and errors on failure
changes: The build/deploy script now updates existing app capabilities instead of creating new ones.
Deployment will stop with an error if required capabilities are still missing after the update.

sources.title: refactor(fastlane/Fastfile): Refactor capability update logic in Fastfile

items.title: Set provisioning team only when needed
changes: The build script now sets the App Store team only when new app capabilities must be added.
The team-selection step is skipped when no capability changes are required.
This change affects only the app build/release automation and not app behavior for users.

sources.title: refactor(fastlane): set current team ID for provisioning request client

items.title: Simplify capability setup in build script
changes: Deployment script now creates missing app capabilities instead of updating them.
The script no longer performs extra checks or error handling after adding capabilities.
The bundle ID configuration no longer includes the USERNOTIFICATIONS_TIMESENSITIVE capability.

sources.title: refactor(fastlane): Refactor capability update logic in Fastfile

includedPullRequests.title: Refactor Trio Alert/Notification Handling – Prepare O5 and Libre 3/3+ Integration [1/3]
includedPullRequests.body: ## Summary Refactors Trio's entire alert / notification handling. Routes all device + glucose alerts through a single `TrioAlertManager` (serves as LoopKit `AlertIssuer`/`AlertResponder`), introduces a user-configurable glucose alarm list (LoopFollow-style), tiered device-alarm list, a shared Day/Night window, and unifies snooze handling across phone / watch / banner / lock-screen quick-action. **Basic functionality:** If user has Trio in the foreground, we display an in-app banner. If Trio is backgrounded, we push a UserNotification. ## Changes - Adds glucose alarms via user-defined list (urgent-low / low / high / forecasted-low / carbs-required), each with own threshold, schedule, sound, per-window enable, snooze. Hierarchical suppression (urgent-low silences low; low family silences forecasted-low). - Adds `CGMManagerAlertOwnership` gate: when the active CGM provides its own glucose alerts (Dexcom G5 / G6 / One / G7 / One+, plus xDrip4iOS via the `.xdrip` App Group source), reading-driven alarms move into a dedicated "Handled by CGM App" section and the coordinator suppresses them. The "Use CGM App Alerts" toggle is auto-off + disabled when the active CGM has no companion app (e.g. LibreLoop), so users see clearly that Trio owns the alerting. The footer names the responsible app and renders the name as a deep link when a URL scheme is known. - Adds device alarms with three severity tiers (Critical / Time-Sensitive / Normal). Each tier holds multiple configs keyed by Day/Night/Always; pump alerts pick the matching variant. Snooze is per-tier. - Adds `AlertCatalogVendor` protocol + `AlertCatalogRegistry` — a per-pump catalog (Omnipod / Minimed / Dana / Medtrum, incl. Omnipod's dynamic `Fault Event Code 0xNN: …` hex parser) that maps each plugin-emitted `Alert.Identifier` to its `(concept, interruptionLevel, title, category)`. Replaces the previous substring classifier; concept-based dedupe collapses cross-plugin duplicates ("Reservoir Empty" etc.) in the Settings UI. - CGM lifecycle alerts (sensor failure / expiry / signal loss) bypass the catalog and pass through with the plugin's chosen interruption level. - Adds configurable Day & Night Window that is shared across alarms; default day starts 06:00, night starts 22:00 (or the AM/PM equivalent). - Adds sound library of 17 bundled `.caf` tones; per-alarm picker with audible preview, override-Silence-&-Focus toggle. Ported from Loop's `next-dev` branch (thanks @ps2). Alarm row surfaces sound on/off and Overrides-Focus as separate inline tags so "silent but haptic-pierces-Focus" is visible. - Adds critical-audio fallback: `CriticalAlertAudioPlayer` plays via `AVAudioSession(.playback)` + `MPVolumeView` for builds without the Critical Alerts entitlement; iOS otherwise silently downgrades and DnD / silent switch mute the sound. Partially taken from PR #392, inspired by both LoopFollow (thanks @bjorkert) and Loop's `next-dev` (thanks @ps2). - Adds `com.apple.developer.usernotifications.time-sensitive` entitlement so `.timeSensitive` alerts pierce Focus modes. - Replaces the legacy "Not-Looping" monitor with a re-armed delayed alert: every loop success cancels and re-schedules a `delayed(20m)` alert via LoopKit's `Alert.Trigger`. Background-survivable; retracts cleanly when loop and/or app recovers. Skipped automatically when closed loop is off, a manual temp basal is active, or the pump is suspended — those states imply the user isn't expecting auto-enactment. - Adds **Carbs Required** as a configurable `GlucoseAlert` type alongside the existing low/high/forecasted family. Configured under `Settings → Glucose Alarms → Carbs Required` with its own threshold (grams), schedule, sound, snooze, and Override Silence & Focus toggle. The alarm threshold is **independent** from the home-screen tab badge's threshold (which lives in `Settings → Features → UI → Carbs Required Threshold`), so users can have a low-threshold passive badge and a higher-threshold active interrupt. Existing installs get the new alarm backfilled on launch alongside any other types added by later releases. - Add-Alarm picker enforces a one-alarm-per-window constraint per type. Types whose Day & Night coverage is fully occupied (either a single `.always` alarm or both `.day` + `.night` set) show up under an `Unavailable` section in the picker with a footer hint pointing at the existing alarm. The editor's "Active During" dropdown is filtered to options that wouldn't collide. - Adds new in-app banner in iOS-push style; stacked deck with critical-always-on-top, tap or swipe up to snooze 15min by default, long-press for context-menu to snooze (15m / 1h / 3h / 6h). ## Test coverage - Per-pump alert-emission + classification: `Omnipod`, `Minimed`, `Dana`, `Medtrum`, `Tandem`, `RileyLink`. - Catalog: `AlertCatalogRegistryEntriesTests`, `AlertCatalogRegistryOmniFaultTests` (hex-prefix parser). - Glucose alarms: `GlucoseAlertTests`, `GlucoseAlertCoordinatorTests` (incl. carbs-required predicate guards), `GlucoseAlertConfigurationTests` (incl. DST spring-forward / fall-back), `ForecastedGlucoseEvaluatorTests`, `GlucoseAlertsStoreTests` (seed + backfill + Add-picker window gating). - Ownership lookup: `CGMManagerAlertOwnershipTests` (manager + `.xdrip` source paths + deep-link mapping). - Plumbing: `AlertMuterTests`, `DeviceAlertsStoreTests` (variant precedence + per-tier snooze), `TrioAlertCategoryTests`, `TrioModalAlertSchedulerTests`, `NotLoopingMonitorTests`. ## Screenshots | Critical Alert | Time-Sensitive Alert | Info Alert | Snoozing | |--------|--------|--------|--------| | | | | | | Day & Night Windows | Glucose Alarms | Device Alarms | Sound Config (w/ Play) | |--------|--------|--------|--------| | | | | | | Device Alarm Form | Device Alarm Form | Glucose Alarm Form | Glucose Alarm Addition | |--------|--------|--------|--------| | | | | | ## Additional notes - Old per-category settings are gone. Alarms now live in the new modules (`Settings → Glucose Alarms / Device Alarms / Day & Night Window`). - Pump alerts no longer auto-acknowledge; they surface through the unified in-app banner + UserNotification pipeline until the user (or the device) clears them. - Critical interruption level pierces snooze; Time-Sensitive / Active are fully suppressed during a snooze window.

The alarm threshold is **independent** from the home-screen tab badge's threshold (which lives in `Settings → Features → UI → Carbs Required Threshold`), so users can have a low-threshold passive badge and a higher-threshold active interrupt. Existing installs get the new alarm backfilled on launch alongside any other types added by later releases. - Add-Alarm picker enforces a one-alarm-per-window constraint per type. Types whose Day & Night coverage is fully occupied (either a single `.always` alarm or both `.day` + `.night` set) show up under an `Unavailable` section in the picker with a footer hint pointing at the existing alarm. The editor's "Active During" dropdown is filtered to options that wouldn't collide. - Adds new in-app banner in iOS-push style; stacked deck with critical-always-on-top, tap or swipe up to snooze 15min by default, long-press for context-menu to snooze (15m / 1h / 3h / 6h). ## Test coverage - Per-pump alert-emission + classification: `Omnipod`, `Minimed`, `Dana`, `Medtrum`, `Tandem`, `RileyLink`. - Catalog: `AlertCatalogRegistryEntriesTests`, `AlertCatalogRegistryOmniFaultTests` (hex-prefix parser). - Glucose alarms: `GlucoseAlertTests`, `GlucoseAlertCoordinatorTests` (incl. carbs-required predicate guards), `GlucoseAlertConfigurationTests` (incl. includedPullRequests.title: Move "Snooze All" to Settings and Clean up Legacy Alerting; Follow-Up to #1203 – Prepare O5 and Libre 3/3+ Integration [3/3]
includedPullRequests.body:
NEEDS TO BE MERGED AFTER #1203 LANDS IN `feat/o5` BRANCH.

## Summary

- **Snooze All** moves from a hidden long-press on the home glucose bobble to a prominent button in **Settings → Notifications**. Tapping it opens an inline sheet with the four canonical durations (15 min / 1 hr / 3 hrs / 6 hrs), each calling `TrioAlertManager.applySnooze(for:)` directly.
- **Snooze module deleted.** No more module / router round-trip, no obsolete `GlucoseAlarm` enum, no stray 20 min option.
- **Legacy SwiftMessages alert pipeline removed.** `Router.alertMessage`, `MessageContent`, `MessageType`, `MessageSubtype`, `NotificationAction`, `alertMessageNotificationObserver`, `MainStateModel`'s SwiftMessages dispatch, and the dead `.pumpConfig` UN-tap deep-link are all gone. `SwiftMessages` SwiftPM dependency dropped.
- **4 real callers migrated** to `TrioAlertManager`:
  - Calibration: "Glucose is stale for calibration"
  - GarminManager: "Garmin Connect must be installed"
  - CGM Settings (×2): "Unable to open the app" / "No URL available"
- **`Logger.info()`** is now log-only — matches `warning` / `debug`. Banner side-effect removed.
- **`TimeInterval` convenience helpers** (`init(hours:)`, `init(minutes:)`, getters, statics) extracted from the deleted Snooze module into `Trio/Sources/Helpers/TimeInterval+Convenience.swift`, since callers outside the alert pipeline rely on them.

Net effect: every user-facing alert in the app flows through `TrioAlertManager`. No more parallel SwiftMessages path.

Follow-up to PR #1203.

includedPullRequests.title: Move "Snooze All" to Settings and Clean up Legacy Alerting; Follow-Up to #1203 – Prepare O5 and Libre 3/3+ Integration [3/3]
includedPullRequests.body:
NEEDS TO BE MERGED AFTER #1203 LANDS IN `feat/o5` BRANCH.

## Summary

- **Snooze All** moves from a hidden long-press on the home glucose bobble to a prominent button in **Settings → Notifications**. Tapping it opens an inline sheet with the four canonical durations (15 min / 1 hr / 3 hrs / 6 hrs), each calling `TrioAlertManager.applySnooze(for:)` directly.
- **Snooze module deleted.** No more module / router round-trip, no obsolete `GlucoseAlarm` enum, no stray 20 min option.
- **Legacy SwiftMessages alert pipeline removed.** `Router.alertMessage`, `MessageContent`, `MessageType`, `MessageSubtype`, `NotificationAction`, `alertMessageNotificationObserver`, `MainStateModel`'s SwiftMessages dispatch, and the dead `.pumpConfig` UN-tap deep-link are all gone. `SwiftMessages` SwiftPM dependency dropped.
- **4 real callers migrated** to `TrioAlertManager`:
  - Calibration: "Glucose is stale for calibration"
  - GarminManager: "Garmin Connect must be installed"
  - CGM Settings (×2): "Unable to open the app" / "No URL available"
- **`Logger.info()`** is now log-only — matches `warning` / `debug`. Banner side-effect removed.
- **`TimeInterval` convenience helpers** (`init(hours:)`, `init(minutes:)`, getters, statics) extracted from the deleted Snooze module into `Trio/Sources/Helpers/TimeInterval+Convenience.swift`, since callers outside the alert pipeline rely on them.

Net effect: every user-facing alert in the app flows through `TrioAlertManager`. No more parallel SwiftMessages path.

Follow-up to PR #1203.

includedPullRequests.title: Add a Quick Bolus feature
includedPullRequests.body: Inspired by @bjorkert 's work on Loop Follow, I have added a Quick Bolus feature. **Why?** Users who pre-bolus and/or do not register carbs still have to go through the Treatments sheet, tap the bolus field, add amount, and confirm. When you "just want to bolus", the Treatments sheet is a bit complex. **How does it work?** Tap and hold the + button to open the Quick Bolus feature. It will look at your bolus history for the last 90 days and suggest up to 5 different amounts that you usually give at the same time-of-day. It will distinguish between weekdays and weekends, and give less weight to older bolus data. **Screenshots** If you have no bolus history, it will let you know: Bolus history of 1U and 2U: Quick Bolus suggests 1U and 2U: More historic values: More suggestions: After selecting a value, you are asked to confirm. Biometric control is applied after confirmation. Information/help sheet:

includedPullRequests.body: Inspired by @bjorkert 's work on Loop Follow, I have added a Quick Bolus feature. **Why?** Users who pre-bolus and/or do not register carbs still have to go through the Treatments sheet, tap the bolus field, add amount, and confirm. When you "just want to bolus", the Treatments sheet is a bit complex. **How does it work?** Tap and hold the + button to open the Quick Bolus feature. It will look at your bolus history for the last 90 days and suggest up to 5 different amounts that you usually give at the same time-of-day. It will distinguish between weekdays and weekends, and give less weight to older bolus data. **Screenshots** If you have no bolus history, it will let you know: Bolus history of 1U and 2U: Quick Bolus suggests 1U and 2U: More historic values: More suggestions: After selecting a value, you are asked to confirm. Biometric control is applied after confirmation. Information/help sheet:

includedPullRequests.title: Use Swift Oref by default
includedPullRequests.body:
This change updates the oref swift setting to be an oref javascript
setting instead, effectively defaulting all users into the Swift
implementation.

By defaulting people into the Swift implementation we will get a
chance to find any lingering issues with the implementation before
removing the Javascript code from the codebase. If there are any
issues, people can switch back to JS without needing to recompile.

includedPullRequests.title: Use Swift Oref by default
includedPullRequests.body:
This change updates the oref swift setting to be an oref javascript
setting instead, effectively defaulting all users into the Swift
implementation.

By defaulting people into the Swift implementation we will get a
chance to find any lingering issues with the implementation before
removing the Javascript code from the codebase. If there are any
issues, people can switch back to JS without needing to recompile.

includedPullRequests.title: Replace Image in GlucoseChartView with symbol
includedPullRequests.body: CGM readings now use Swift Charts' built-in `.symbol(.circle)` instead of a custom `.symbol { Image(...) } `closure. Custom symbol views make Charts host a full SwiftUI view hierarchy per mark. Visually nothing has changed.
